content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA with NNN non-negative integers. There are NNN bars arranged adjacent to each other, such that the height of the ithi^{th}ith bar is AiA_iAi​ units and the width of each bar is 111 unit.

Find out the total amount of water the bars can trap after raining.

**Note**: Use 64 bit integers.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers - the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the total amount of water the bars can trap after raining.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of bars. The second line contains N space‑separated non‑negative integers A1 … AN, the heights of the bars.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the total amount of water that can be trapped after raining. The answer fits in a 64‑bit signed integer.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: Bars [2,1,1,3] trap 2 units of water.
Test case 2: Bars [4,1,1,1] trap no water.
Test case 3: Bars [2,2] trap no water.
Test case 4: Bars [3,0,2,0,4] trap 7 units of water.
The classic example where the trapped water amounts to 9 units.
Only the middle zero can hold water, bounded by height 2 on both sides.

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the array with two pointers left and right. We keep the maximum height seen so far from the left (leftMax) and from the right (rightMax). At each step we move the pointer with the smaller current height because the water level at that position is limited by the smaller side. The trapped water at that index is max(0, leftMax - height) or max(0, rightMax - height) depending on which side we process. This yields O(N) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the size of the array. Overall O(sum of N) ≤ O(2·10^5).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the input array.
